LAW AND MOTION 301, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION: THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: THE HARD DISK OF EVERY COMPUTER UTILIZED BY EVERY DEFENDANT (HOME AND BUSINESS) IN ITS PRESENT CONDITION WILL BE PROFESSIONALLY COPIED (1 COPY). COUNSEL ARE TO AGREE ON THE METHOD TO BE USED. ALL COSTS OF COPYING WILL BE BORNE BY PLAINTIFF NALCO COMPANY; DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO COOPERATE BY MAKING THE COMPUTERS AVAILABLE. THE CURRENT PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, IN ITS PRESENT FORM, WILL REMAIN IN EFFECT. ONCE THE DISKS ARE COPIED, THE COURT WILL CONSIDER MODIFICATION OF THE INJUNCTION ORDER. THE COURT DIRECTS COUNSEL TO SUBMIT THE DISKS TO THE COURT, AS PROVIDED BELOW. THE COURT ALSO DIRECTS COUNSL TO DRAFT, WITH APPROVAL AS TO FORM, A PROPOSED MODIFIED INJUNCTION WHICH SHOULD BE SUBMITTED TO THE COURT. THE DISKS WILL BE DEPOSITED WITH THE COURT, UNDER SEAL AND BY PROTECTIVE ORDER, FOR SAFEKEEPING PURPOSES; THE DISKS WILL BE RETAINED PENDING THE OUTCOME OF DEFENDANTS' SLAPP MOTION OR UNTIL DISCOVERY IS RESOLVED. MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patricia H. Cullison, Esq. and Angelina Shepardson, Esq., Seyfarth Shaw LLP (415) 397-2823, for Petitioner and Moving Party Nalco Company
Stanley G. Hilton, Esq. (408) 273-4593, for Respondents and Opposing Party Orsiris Water, O'Connell and Binfield
Steven Duditch, Esq. (415) 456-3422, for Respondent and Opposing Party Shaw
RULING:
A R G U E D; ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION: The Court rules as follows: The hard disk of every computer utilized by every Defendant (home and business) in its present condition will be professionally copied (1 copy). Counsel are to agree on the method to be used. All costs of copying will be borne by Plaintiff Nalco Company; Defendants are ordered to cooperate by making the computers available.
The current preliminary injunction, in its present form, will remain in effect. Once the disks are copied, the Court will consider modification of the injunction order. The Court directs Counsel to submit the disks to the Court, as provided below. The Court also directs Counsl to draft, with approval as to form, a proposed modified injunction which should be submitted to the Court.
The disks will be deposited with the Court, under seal and by protective order, for safekeeping purposes; the disks will be retained pending the outcome of Defendants' SLAPP motion or until discovery is resolved. (301/JLW/AA) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
